Adding and Removing Friends

Xbox Live titles must allow players to add and remove players from the friends list. Membership on the friends list is mutual: if player A is on player B's friends list, player B must also be on player A's friends list. Also, the maximum number of users on the friends list is limited to the value of the MAX_FRIENDS constant, defined in XOnline.h. As a result of these two constraints, a player whose friends list is full is not able to add new players, and is also not able to be added to other players' friends lists.

Use the following functions when adding or removing player's from the friends list.

Function Description Memory Use
XOnlineFriendsRequest Requests that a player be added to a friends list. Titles can also use XOnlineFriendsRequestEx and XOnlineFriendsRequestByNameEx, which allow the title to include voice attachments with the friend request. No.
XOnlineFriendsAnswerRequest Responds to a request generated by XOnlineFriendsRequest. Yes—allocates memory for a request to the service.
XOnlineFriendsRemove Removes a player from the friends list. Yes—allocates memory for an XONLINE_FRIEND structure.

Adding Friends: Sending and Receiving Friend Requests

Xbox Live titles use the XOnlineFriendsRequest function to request that a new player be added to the current player's friends list. After calling XOnlineFriendsRequest, the title should call XOnlineTaskContinue with the handle returned by XOnlineFriendsStartup in order to process the request.

The player who is the recipient of the request receives a notification of type XONLINE_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_FRIEND_REQUEST in his or her notification queue. The title running on the Xbox console of the receiving player should periodically call the XOnlineGetNotification function for each of the players on that Xbox console to check for the receipt of such notifications. The title should then inform the player that he or she has a notification pending.

To determine the identity of the player who sent the request, the receiving title enumerates the friends list for the receiving player. The requesting player is present in the receiving player's friends list with a friend state of XONLINE_FRIENDSTATE_FLAG_RECEIVEDREQUEST. (The receiving player is also present in the requesting player's friends list with a friend state of XONLINE_FRIENDSTATE_FLAG_SENTREQUEST.) The friend state is stored in the Friendstate member of the XONLINE_FRIEND structure.

The title on the receiving player's Xbox console should call XOnlineFriendsAnswerRequest with an appropriate value from XONLINE_REQUEST_ANSWER_TYPE. Next, it pumps the task handle by calling XOnlineTaskContinue with the handle returned by XOnlineFriendsStartup.

If the receiving player accepts the request to be added to the friends list, the title should respond with XONLINE_REQUEST_YES. If the player declines the request, the response should be XONLINE_REQUEST_NO. The receiving player also has the option to specify that he or she no longer wants to receive friends requests from the requesting player. In this case, the title should use the XONLINE_REQUEST_BLOCK value.

The friends system does not send notifications to the requester indicating whether the requestee accepted, rejected, or blocked the request. If the requestee accepts the request, the status of the requestee in the requester's list changes to friend. Likewise, the status of the requester in the requestee's list also changes to friend. If the receiving player declines the request or blocks further requests, the two players simply disappear from each other's friends lists. When this occurs, your title can, for example, display a polite message indicating that the request has been declined.

Because the friend request is an actual message, your title can attach voice or text to it. The fact that actual messages are not sent from the requestee to the requester means that voice and text are not, by default, sent back to the requester. However, this does not prohibit your title from implementing them. For example, suppose you want to implement voice and text attachments to an acceptance message. In that case, your title would go through the normal process of accepting a friend request. It would then generate a title custom message to which the requestee can attach voice or text and transmit this message to the requester.

If the recipient of a friend request blocks the requesting player (XONLINE_REQUEST_BLOCK) and subsequently sends a friend request to that blocked player, the blocked player becomes unblocked. The blocked player is unblocked regardless of his or her response to the friend request.

User Interface Considerations

A straightforward way for titles to provide the functionality for adding new friends is to enable a player to add other players from the current game session. In this scenario, the title already has access to the information needed to add the player to the friends list. If the player is on the local Xbox console, the title can obtain the information through the XOnlineGetLogonUsers function. If the player is on a remote Xbox console, the information should be available from the Xbox console that is hosting the online-game session. For more information on network communication between Xbox consoles, see Xbox Secure Network Library.

The Xbox Dashboard screen for the friends list supports a virtual keyboard that can be used to add friends based on user name. The Xbox Dashboard can be considered the default means for adding friends to the friends list.

Removing Friends

Use the XOnlineFriendsRemove function to remove a player from the friends list. The title should provide an opportunity for the player who requests removal to confirm that he or she wants to proceed with the operation. Because friends list membership is mutual, the player who requests removal is also removed from the friends list of the target player. Removal results in the target player receiving a XONLINE_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_FRIENDREQUEST notification. However, the title should not display this notification to the player. Each player should silently disappear from the other's list.

As with adding friends, the Xbox Dashboard screen for the friends list supports removing friends as well.
